name: An attempt was made to reset an account's password
applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 R2 and later; Windows 7 and later
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4724 records an attempt to reset another account's password and is important for account-takeover investigations.
trigger_scenarios: The event is logged when an administrator, helpdesk workflow, or other principal resets a user password rather than changing its own password.

key_fields.explanation: The identity that attempted the reset. Confirm it is an approved administrator or identity-management process.
The account whose password was reset. Privileged, service, and recently created accounts deserve special attention.
The operation result. Failed resets can reveal attempted but blocked account abuse.

false_positives: Helpdesk and self-service identity workflows legitimately reset passwords.
Emergency access procedures may reset privileged accounts during incidents.
detection_notes: T1098 Account Manipulation is signaled when Subject resets a privileged or service Target Account and Status is 0x0, followed by 4624 from a new Client Address. A successful reset outside approved helpdesk workflows gives an attacker a new valid credential, unlike a failed policy attempt.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4724
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TargetAccount, Activity, Status
| order by TimeGenerated desc
spl_snippet:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4724
| table _time, host, SubjectUserName, TargetUserName, Status
sample_log:
Subject: CORP\helpdesk01
Target Account: CORP\admin.ops
Status: 0x0

queries.title: Retrieve Event ID 4724
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4724
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Microsoft Sentinel Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Apply the event-specific field and correlation guidance on this page after retrieving the base events.

queries.title: Retrieve Event ID 4724
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4724
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and field aliases vary by Splunk deployment and Windows add-on version.
Map provider fields from the raw event before implementing event-specific filtering or aggregation.

queries.title: Retrieve Event ID 4724
assumptions: The relevant Windows event channel is ingested through a Cortex XDR Windows event collection path.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed field names vary by collection path and event provider.

queries.title: Windows Event ID 4724
queries.query:
title: Windows Event ID 4724
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4724
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the field conditions and correlations documented on this page in the destination SIEM.

value_references.security_relevance: A successful reset of a privileged or service account outside an approved workflow can give an actor durable access with a credential they control.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4724 mean?
faqs.answer: Event ID 4724 records an attempt by one account to reset another account's password and can be generated for successful or failed attempts.

faqs.question: How is a password reset different from a password change?
faqs.answer: A reset is performed without supplying the target account's old password, while event 4723 covers an attempt to change an account password using the existing credential flow.

faqs.question: Which 4724 events deserve immediate review?
faqs.answer: Prioritize successful resets of privileged, service, dormant, or break-glass accounts by an unexpected subject, especially when followed by a logon from a new source.

sources.title: 4724: Microsoft Windows security auditing event reference
